title: Claim an overseas State Pension if you’ve lived or worked abroad
body: "You may be able to claim an overseas state pension before you’re UK State Pension age. Your eligibility will depend on that country’s rules.  If you lived or worked in a European Economic Area (EEA) country or Switzerland, you can check the European Commission website to find out:    the pension age for the country you lived or worked in   how long you need to have lived or worked in the country to be eligible    To make a claim for a foreign pension, please complete and return this form to the International Pension Centre in Newcastle using the freepost address.   You must only use this form if you have lived or worked in one of the countries listed on this page. To claim state pension from a country that is not named on this page, apply to their pension scheme directly.   You need to tell us about the following countries if you have lived in any of them:   Denmark Finland Iceland Liechtenstein Netherlands Norway Sweden   You need to tell us about the following countries if you have worked in any of them:   Austria Belgium Bulgaria Croatia Cyprus Czech Republic Estonia France Germany Gibraltar Greece Hungary Ireland Italy Latvia Lithuania Luxembourg Malta Poland Portugal Romania Slovakia Slovenia Spain Switzerland   What happens next  We will send the information you give us to the relevant foreign authorities. They will decide if you are entitled to a pension from them, and will write to you to tell you their decision.  If you need help to complete this form, contact the International Pension Centre. "
